Valer..personality: A highly excitable, nervous temperament most often seen in women and children, prone to hysterical and hypochondriacal states. The person is restless and cannot remain still, feeling compelled to move about, with complaints that constantly shift and wander from place to place.
Valer..mental_state: The mind is in a state of extreme activity and tension, rapidly shifting between subjects and ideas with an ecstatic or hysterical quality. There are erroneous perceptions of identity—believing herself to be someone else, seeing imaginary animals and figures, especially at night. Mental symptoms arise during rest and in darkness, clearing when the person walks about.
Valer..emotional_pattern: Deep sadness alternates with irritability and a morose, easily exasperated disposition. There is pronounced fear in the evening and in the dark, with an underlying hypersensitivity to all stimuli. Emotional states are unstable and paroxysmal, flaring during stillness and solitude, and temporarily relieved by motion and activity.
